specific_location: Along irrigation canal between West Lake St. and West Prospect St., about 0.5 mi. west of Taft Hill Rd.
description: A small warbler-sized passerine with a short, thick "vireo" bill. Underparts tinged with yellow, brightest on the throat and vent. Gray above with green tinge on back, blue-gray crown, whitish eyeline above and around eye, with darker gray lores. Short tail relative to other vireos. Wings plain without obvious wing bars. Wing coverts with narrow pale edges visible at certain angles. Tertials edged whitish, and primaries dark gray (almost black) with pale edges.Behaviors: Flitting in trees from 10 - 25 feet above ground, gleaning food among leaves, particularly in Hackberry trees with obvious psyllid infestations, and often in direct sunlight.Call: nonePlumage: adult
photographer: Photographer: Nick Komar, Nick Komar, Nick Komar, Nick Komar, Nick Komar
